title: Module 1
content:
Introduction- stress, strain and its types, stress strain diagram (ductile &
brittle materials), material properties, Relationship between elastic
constants (E, G & v).
Calculation of stress, strain and change in length in axially loaded
members with single and composite materials, Effects of thermal loading –
thermal stress and thermal strain

title: Module 2
content:
Method of sections, Stress vectors on Cartesian coordinate planes passing
through a point, stress at a point in the form of a matrix. Equality of cross
shear, Cauchy's equation (no derivation). Principal planes and principal
stress, meaning of stress invariants, maximum shear stress. Mohr’s circle
for 2D cases
Displacement, gradient of displacement, Cartesian strain matrix, strain-
displacement relations (small-strain only), Simple problems to find strain
matrix.

title: Module 3
content:
Torsional deformation of circular shafts, assumptions for shafts subjected
to torsion within elastic deformation range, torsion formula (no
derivation). Torsional rigidity, Polar moment of inertia, basic design of
transmission shafts.
Types of beams, loading and supports. Shear force and bending moment
diagrams for cantilever and simply supported beams (point load & UDL).

title: Module 4
content:
Fundamentals of buckling and stability, critical load,  Buckling of columns
with pinned ends, Euler’s buckling theory for long columns. Critical stress,
slenderness ratio, Rankine’s formula for short columns.
Theories of Failure, Rankine’s theory for maximum normal stress, Guest’s
theory for maximum shear stress, Saint-Venant’s theory for maximum
normal strain, Hencky-von Mises theory for maximum distortion energy,
Haigh’s theory for maximum strain energy
